sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: The upcoming clash between Las Palmas and Valencia is set for May 3, 2025, at 16:30 in the iconic Gran Canaria stadium in Las Palmas de Gran Canaria. This Round 34 encounter pits a struggling Las Palmas, currently 18th with 32 points, against a mid-table Valencia, positioned 14th with 39 points. With both teams needing points for different reasons, this promises to be a tense battle. The referee, Ricardo De Burgos Bengoetxea, is known for averaging 4.7 yellow cards and 0.19 red cards per game, suggesting a potentially physical matchup.

sections.subtitle: Recent Head-to-Head Encounters
sections.text: Looking at the last 5 matchups between these sides, Valencia has secured 3 wins while Las Palmas has claimed 2 victories, with no draws recorded. Their most recent meeting on October 21, 2024, saw Las Palmas pull off a thrilling 3-2 upset away at Valencia, with Fábio Silva earning Player of the Match honors with a 7.7 Sofascore rating. Historically, Valencia has been first to score in 8 of their last 10 head-to-heads, indicating their tendency to start strong against Las Palmas.

sections.subtitle: Team Form and Streaks
sections.text: Valencia enters this match with an impressive streak of no losses in their last 8 games across all competitions, often scoring first in 6 of their last 8 matches. Meanwhile, Las Palmas has struggled, being the first to concede in 6 of their last 7 games and losing at halftime in 5 of their last 6 matches. Both teams have trends of low corner counts, with Las Palmas seeing under 10.5 corners in their last 6 games and Valencia in 8 of their last 10 matches.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: For Las Palmas, Sandro Ramírez stands out with 8 goals this season and an impressive xG overperformance of 4.8888, showing clinical finishing. José Campaña, ranked 1st in the league for aerial duels won at 81%, will be crucial in midfield battles. Juanma Herzog, with a 70.8% ground duel win rate (5th in the league), adds defensive solidity. On Valencia’s side, Hugo Duro leads with 8 goals, while Pepelu, who has averaged a 7.24 Sofascore rating against Las Palmas over the last 5 matchups with 2 goals, remains a consistent threat. Yarek Gasiorowski also impresses with a 71% ground duel win rate, ranked 4th league-wide.

sections.subtitle: Statistical Insights
sections.text: Las Palmas has scored 38 goals this season, ranking 10th in the league, but their defensive frailties are evident with 53 goals conceded, placing them 19th for least goals conceded. They excel in ground duels, ranked 5th with a 51.07% win rate, and average 50.3% ball possession (10th in the league). Valencia, meanwhile, has shown efficiency in front of goal, particularly between the 31st and 45th minutes, where 28.57% of their goals are scored. Their ability to create big chances early could exploit Las Palmas’ tendency to concede first.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Battle
sections.text: Las Palmas will likely rely on late-game surges, with 22.64% of their goals coming between the 76th and 90th minutes. However, missing key midfielder Kirian Rodríguez (average rating of 7.0) could hamper their creativity. Valencia, with Giorgi Mamardashvili in fine form over the last 5 matches (average rating of 7.5), has a solid backbone in goal. Their ability to score first (6 of last 8 games) could put early pressure on a Las Palmas side that struggles in the first half.

sections.subtitle: Discipline and Set-Pieces
sections.text: Discipline could play a big role, with Las Palmas averaging 2.91 yellow cards per game and ranking 20th for least yellow cards received (96 total). Valencia’s games also often see fewer than 4.5 cards in recent matches, suggesting a potentially cautious approach. On set-pieces, Las Palmas ranks 17th with just 3.82 corners per game, while both teams have seen under 10.5 corners in most recent head-to-heads (7 of last 8 encounters), pointing to limited action from dead balls.
